com.mentor.chs.api.report

Interface IXDesignComparisonContext


  • public interface IXDesignComparisonContext
    Design Compariosn context allows
    1) Registration of user defined comparators and matchers
    2)Definition of the colors to be used in diagram ouputs, for example in SVG diagrams to represent a change
    3)Procure the design comparison factory, which in turn can be used for creation of some default implementations and the objects meant for holding the change, like IXChangedObject, IXChangeType and IXChangeDescription.
    After creation of the compartor IXObjectComparator and matcher IXObjectMatcheruser has to register it with the comparison context. The matchers will be used for matching the objects in current and reference design and identifying the pair meant for comparison.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.String getColorCodeForAdd()
      Color code that is to be used for depicting the Added objects
      java.lang.String getColorCodeForChange()
      Color code that is to be used for depicting the Changed objects
      java.lang.String getColorCodeForRemove()
      Color code that is to be used for depicting the Removed objects
      java.util.Collection<IXObjectComparator> getComparators()
      Get the comparators registered by the user.
      IXDesignComparisonFactory getDesignComparisonFactory()
      The handle to the design compariosn factory can be used to create several type of objects used in Comparison
      java.util.Collection<IXObjectMatcher> getMatchers()
      Get the matchers registered by the user.
      void registerComparator(IXObjectComparator c)
      lets users register their own comparators to be used when gathering object and then in deciding whats different about them.
      void registerMatcher(IXObjectMatcher m)
      lets users register their own object matchers to decide if 2 objects are in fact equal when doing the initial build up of objects before they get passed onto the actual comparators
      void setAddedRemovedAndChangedColors(java.lang.String added, java.lang.String removed, java.lang.String changed)
      The components in the current design and not in the reference design are identified as added components.

    • Method Detail

      • registerComparator

        void registerComparator(IXObjectComparator c)
        lets users register their own comparators to be used when gathering object and then in deciding whats different about them.
        Parameters:
        c - a new comparator to use

      • registerMatcher

        void registerMatcher(IXObjectMatcher m)
        lets users register their own object matchers to decide if 2 objects are in fact equal when doing the initial build up of objects before they get passed onto the actual comparators
        Parameters:
        m - the matcher to add

      • setAddedRemovedAndChangedColors

        void setAddedRemovedAndChangedColors(java.lang.String added,
                                     java.lang.String removed,
                                     java.lang.String changed)
        The components in the current design and not in the reference design are identified as added components. Removed components are those that are not in the current design but present in the reference design. Changed components are those that are present in both the designs.
        Parameters:
        added - the color code to be used for displaying the added components in current design.
        removed - the color code to be used for displaying the removed components in current design.
        changed - the color code to be used for displaying the changed components in current design.

      • getDesignComparisonFactory

        IXDesignComparisonFactory getDesignComparisonFactory()
        The handle to the design compariosn factory can be used to create several type of objects used in Comparison
        Returns:
        handle to the design comparison factory.

      • getColorCodeForRemove

        java.lang.String getColorCodeForRemove()
        Color code that is to be used for depicting the Removed objects
        Returns:
        color code set for removed objects. Null otherwise.

      • getColorCodeForChange

        java.lang.String getColorCodeForChange()
        Color code that is to be used for depicting the Changed objects
        Returns:
        color code set for changed objects. Null otherwise.

      • getColorCodeForAdd

        java.lang.String getColorCodeForAdd()
        Color code that is to be used for depicting the Added objects
        Returns:
        color code set for added objects. Null otherwise.

      • getComparators

        java.util.Collection<IXObjectComparator> getComparators()
        Get the comparators registered by the user.
        Returns:
        collection of all the comparators registered by the user.

      • getMatchers

        java.util.Collection<IXObjectMatcher> getMatchers()
        Get the matchers registered by the user.
        Returns:
        collection of all the matchers registered by the user.
